counting_justification:
Study classified as primary because it presents new genotyping results for 46 unrelated individuals with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM). (DOCUMENT, Methods/Results sections).

Variant identification: The target variant is MYL2 p.Arg58Gln (c.173G>A). It is explicitly reported in section 3.3 (“…a known missense mutation (Arg58Gln)…”) and in Table 1 (Individual XII, Gene MYL2 Arg 58 Gln). The exact strings “Arg58Gln” and “Arg 58 Gln” were captured as variant_forms. No other aliases were found in the text.

Phenotype mapping: The proband (Individual XII) is described as having hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM), an ALLOWED_PHENOTYPE per GUIDE. The 18-year-old granddaughter is also affected but belongs to the same pedigree; by default related individuals are not counted separately. The 44-year-old daughter is genotype-positive but clinically unaffected; she is not used for PS4 counting because controls must come from non-enriched cohorts and related individuals can bias case-control calculations.

Counting per case-control mode: The publication does not include a formal control cohort for Arg58Gln; therefore b, c, d remain 0. At least one unrelated affected carrier (the proband) is documented, so a = 1. Independence is satisfied because the study states that each of the 46 index cases is unrelated (“46 unrelated individuals” – DOCUMENT, first paragraph of Results). Thus total_ps4_case_counts = 1.

No overlaps with other cohorts within this paper or with external papers were identified.

No exclusions triggered; inheritance is autosomal dominant (GUIDE header), heterozygous carrier assumed unless otherwise stated.
